rationale: Both correctly pinpoint the None+1 crash on the missing <img_ratio_33..36> tokens in hunyuan_image3.py and confirm the same dit.yaml workaround and close-with-follow-up disposition, closely tracking the thread. X more precisely reproduces the thread's actual root-cause narrative (PR #2713 removing the old `modes` filter that let Base skip the AR stage) and proposes exactly the fail-fast guard the thread asked for. Y omits the PR #2713 regression story and instead proposes a 'tolerant/degrade gracefully' code path alongside the fail-fast guard, which is more speculative and risks masking that the AR stage simply shouldn't run for the Base checkpoint — plus several very specific claims (YAML headers, KV-reuse details) aren't verifiable against the ground truth. Both are well-grounded in the actual crashing code, but X aligns marginally closer to what was actually established in the thread.
